name: dlq_kms_master_key_id
description: The ID of an AWS-managed customer master key (CMK) for Amazon SQS or a custom CMK

name: source_dlq_queue_policy_documents
description: List of IAM policy documents that are merged together into the exported document. Statements must have unique `sid`s

name: fifo_queue
description: Boolean designating a FIFO queue

name: use_name_prefix
description: Determines whether `name` is used as a prefix

name: sqs_managed_sse_enabled
description: Boolean to enable server-side encryption (SSE) of message content with SQS-owned encryption keys

name: create_dlq_queue_policy
description: Whether to create SQS queue policy

name: tags
description: A mapping of tags to assign to all resources

name: max_message_size
description: The limit of how many bytes a message can contain before Amazon SQS rejects it. An integer from 1024 bytes (1 KiB) up to 1048576 bytes (1024 KiB). The default for this attribute is 262144 (256 KiB)

name: dlq_tags
description: A mapping of additional tags to assign to the dead letter queue

name: redrive_policy
description: The JSON policy to set up the Dead Letter Queue, see AWS docs. Note: when specifying maxReceiveCount, you must specify it as an integer (5), and not a string ("5")

name: dlq_name
description: This is the human-readable name of the queue. If omitted, Terraform will assign a random name

name: dlq_receive_wait_time_seconds
description: The time for which a ReceiveMessage call will wait for a message to arrive (long polling) before returning. An integer from 0 to 20 (seconds)

name: dlq_redrive_allow_policy
description: The JSON policy to set up the Dead Letter Queue redrive permission, see AWS docs

name: create
description: Whether to create SQS queue

name: delay_seconds
description: The time in seconds that the delivery of all messages in the queue will be delayed. An integer from 0 to 900 (15 minutes)

name: message_retention_seconds
description: The number of seconds Amazon SQS retains a message. Integer representing seconds, from 60 (1 minute) to 1209600 (14 days)

name: redrive_allow_policy
description: The JSON policy to set up the Dead Letter Queue redrive permission, see AWS docs

name: visibility_timeout_seconds
description: The visibility timeout for the queue. An integer from 0 to 43200 (12 hours)

name: dlq_fifo_throughput_limit
description: Specifies whether the Dead Letter Queue FIFO queue throughput quota applies to the entire queue or per message group

name: dlq_queue_policy_statements
description: A map of IAM policy [statements](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/data-sources/iam_policy_document#statement) for custom permission usage

name: deduplication_scope
description: Specifies whether message deduplication occurs at the message group or queue level

name: fifo_throughput_limit
description: Specifies whether the FIFO queue throughput quota applies to the entire queue or per message group

name: override_queue_policy_documents
description: List of IAM policy documents that are merged together into the exported document. In merging, statements with non-blank `sid`s will override statements with the same `sid`

name: create_dlq_redrive_allow_policy
description: Determines whether to create a redrive allow policy for the dead letter queue

name: dlq_sqs_managed_sse_enabled
description: Boolean to enable server-side encryption (SSE) of message content with SQS-owned encryption keys

name: receive_wait_time_seconds
description: The time for which a ReceiveMessage call will wait for a message to arrive (long polling) before returning. An integer from 0 to 20 (seconds)

name: dlq_content_based_deduplication
description: Enables content-based deduplication for FIFO queues

name: dlq_delay_seconds
description: The time in seconds that the delivery of all messages in the queue will be delayed. An integer from 0 to 900 (15 minutes)

name: dlq_message_retention_seconds
description: The number of seconds Amazon SQS retains a message. Integer representing seconds, from 60 (1 minute) to 1209600 (14 days)

name: region
description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration

name: content_based_deduplication
description: Enables content-based deduplication for FIFO queues

name: name
description: This is the human-readable name of the queue. If omitted, Terraform will assign a random name

name: source_queue_policy_documents
description: List of IAM policy documents that are merged together into the exported document. Statements must have unique `sid`s

name: queue_policy_statements
description: A map of IAM policy [statements](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/data-sources/iam_policy_document#statement) for custom permission usage

name: dlq_visibility_timeout_seconds
description: The visibility timeout for the queue. An integer from 0 to 43200 (12 hours)

name: override_dlq_queue_policy_documents
description: List of IAM policy documents that are merged together into the exported document. In merging, statements with non-blank `sid`s will override statements with the same `sid`

name: create_dlq
description: Determines whether to create SQS dead letter queue

name: dlq_deduplication_scope
description: Specifies whether message deduplication occurs at the message group or queue level

name: dlq_kms_data_key_reuse_period_seconds
description: The length of time, in seconds, for which Amazon SQS can reuse a data key to encrypt or decrypt messages before calling AWS KMS again. An integer representing seconds, between 60 seconds (1 minute) and 86,400 seconds (24 hours)

name: kms_data_key_reuse_period_seconds
description: The length of time, in seconds, for which Amazon SQS can reuse a data key to encrypt or decrypt messages before calling AWS KMS again. An integer representing seconds, between 60 seconds (1 minute) and 86,400 seconds (24 hours)

name: kms_master_key_id
description: The ID of an AWS-managed customer master key (CMK) for Amazon SQS or a custom CMK

name: create_queue_policy
description: Whether to create SQS queue policy
